All these broken windows and open doors make it way too easy to grab whatever is left.
The chaos from the flood is the perfect cover for me to slip into those luxury apartments.
I need to find where the emergency supplies are being stored before the guards start patrolling.
Nobody is watching their bags while they scramble to get away from the rising water.
These evacuated houses are just sitting here waiting for someone to take the jewelry and electronics.
I should use this blinding light as a distraction to slip through the jeweler's side door.
The guards are all staring at the sky, leaving the vault completely unattended for once.
If the city panics over that comet, the chaos will make my getaway much easier.
I wonder if that strange glowing rock falling from the sky is made of gold.
Everyone is too mesmerized by the eclipse to notice me picking this lock right now.
I don't care where the line is as long as the fence stays broken.
Your garden tools are already in my shed, so we can't really move back.
I noticed your lock is rusted, which makes this whole boundary talk quite irrelevant.
Why argue about a few feet of dirt when your silver is just sitting there?
Keep shouting about the property line and I'll make sure your jewelry vanishes tonight.
I wonder if the treasures on the other side are guarded by locks I can actually pick.
This shimmering gateway looks expensive, but I cannot figure out how to pry the frame from the wall.
I will slip through quietly and see if there are any unattended purses or jewelry boxes waiting.
If I can sneak in and out quickly, I might find something rare enough to retire on.
My fingers are itching to see what kind of valuables are hidden behind a rift in space.
I must tread lightly through these ferns to avoid leaving any footprints for the trackers.
This dense foliage is perfect for concealing my movements while I scout for the camp.
I can hear their guards chatting nearby, so I will wait in the brush.
My fingers are itching to see if those traveling merchants left their packs unguarded.
I will circle around this thicket to approach the supply wagon from the blind side.
I must hold my breath and stay frozen until the guards stop patrolling this corridor.
Every second of this heavy silence makes me wonder if the alarm has already been triggered.
I can hear my own heart hammering against my ribs while I wait in this darkness.
The longer it stays quiet, the more I worry that someone is lurking just around the corner.
I will keep my hand on the loot and wait for the house to fall asleep.
Put that weapon down and I will leave the jewelry right where it is.
I only wanted the silver, so there is no need for this to get violent.
Just let me walk out the door and I will not take another single thing.
You do not have to do this if you just let me slip away quietly.
I can see you are shaking, so just step back and let me go now.
I have been watching this gold idol for days to find the blind spot in the guard's patrol.
The lock on this velvet case is complex, but my picks will have it open in seconds.
This diamond is far too heavy to carry while climbing down the rain pipe in the dark.
One wrong step on these creaky floorboards and the whole house will wake up and catch me.
I only need to grab the sapphire and slip out the window before the clock strikes midnight.
The guard is distracted by the commotion, so now is my chance to snatch the remaining fuel cells.
I only have a few minutes before the patrol returns to secure the vault of gold coins.
My bag is nearly full, but I cannot leave behind those precious gems while the coast is clear.
I must sneak past the sleeping sentries to grab the last of the water skins from the camp.
There is only one key left for the iron gate, and I intend to have it in my pocket.
